name: contact
description: An identity card that encodes a vCard: name, title and contact lines beside a QR that saves the presenter to a phone.
purpose: Use as the "scan to add me" close or a speaker-intro slide. The QR encodes a vCard the audience saves in one tap; the card shows the name as hero with title/org and a contact ledger. Author the fields as a postfix-key list.

title.description: Optional framing heading; the person's name is the visual hero, drawn from the `name` field.

fields.description: One field per bullet in postfix-key form — value first, trailing inline-code names the field: `- Sharmarke Aden `name``. Keys: name (required), title|role, org|company, email, phone|tel, url|web.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: contact -->

## Add me.

- Full Name `name`
- Title `title`
- Organization `org`
- name@example.com `email`
sample:
<!-- _class: contact -->

## Add me.

- Sharmarke Aden `name`
- Founder & CEO `title`
- SlideWright `org`
- sharmarke@slidewright.dev `email`
- +1-555-0142 `phone`
- slidewright.dev `url`

whenToUse.title: The scan-to-add-me close
whenToUse.body: End a pitch or intro with a code the audience scans to save your contact, instead of trading business cards.

whenToUse.title: Show what you choose, encode the rest
whenToUse.body: Every non-empty field goes into the vCard; the card foregrounds the name, title and org and lists email/phone/url beneath.

antiPatterns.title: Not a team roster
antiPatterns.body: One card is one person. For a set of people use an inventory layout; a contact card is a single identity.

antiPatterns.title: Don't bold the field values
antiPatterns.body: The value leads and the key is a trailing inline-code tag — `- Sharmarke Aden `name``. Don't write `- **Name:** Sharmarke Aden`; the transform reads the postfix key, not a bold label.

related.name: wifi
related.when: the card is a network to join rather than a person
